name: structure-interface
description: Structural and interface FISH intrinsic families are used to extract forces, moments, link state, interface contact response, and reinforcement state after solving.

notes: Structural node values are central for support displacement and link-state checks.
Use element-specific families for cable, beam, pile, shell, liner, and geogrid quantities.
Use the corresponding structural-properties reference before setting element properties.
Interface data are often node based; inspect active contact state before interpreting forces.
Interface stiffness and strength should be validated against adjacent zone stiffness and expected discontinuity behavior.

common_checks: Maximum cable axial load compared with yield capacity.
Liner or shell force envelopes after staged excavation.
Link attachment state after geometry updates or large-strain motion.
